title: hippuris vulgaris f. lacunarum
intro: Hippuris vulgaris f. lacunarum, also known as marsh trefoil or lacustrine mare's tail, is a subspecies of the Hippuris vulgaris plant, a perennial aquatic plant with long, thin leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. Its health benefits include treating kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and other conditions related to the urinary system. Hippuris vulgaris f. lacunarum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in herbal medicine for its diuretic and antiseptic properties.
